images.altText: Smoke rising over a populated hill in the Lebanese countryside.

articles.title: Trump repeats he is 'not happy with Australia' as Hormuz crisis continues

children.content: Donald Trump again accuses Australia of refusing to help the US in the Strait of Hormuz, but the Australian government maintains there has been no formal request from the US.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says

children.content: The US president says he "had excellent conversations" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

image.altText: A large smoke plume erupting from a residential area.

articles.title: 100m litres of diesel secured under new powers, PM confirms

children.content: Anthony Albanese says that new powers allowing the federal government to underwrite the delivery of additional fuel cargoes have been used to purchase 100 million litres of diesel.

image.altText: Two men sit at desk in front of a row of flags.
Night sky of Beirut.

children.content: The US State Department says a 10-day ceasefire will enable negotiations toward a permanent security and peace agreement. Follow live.

items.title: Celebrations mark ceasefire in Lebanon, as Netanyahu says Israel will stay put

updates.title: 🎥 Fireworks light up Beirut as ceasefire with Israel begins
Iran welcomes ceasefire agreement in Lebanon, state media reports

items.title: A 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins: What to know today

image.altText: Man standing in front of cityscape

children.content: Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire, but Israel's military will remain on Lebanese territory, meaning Hezbollah's conditions were not met.

items.title: PM preparing Australia for protracted Middle East conflict

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese insists Australia's sanctions on Russia and Iran will remain in place as the world scrambles to secure oil supplies amid a global crisis triggered by the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese seated for an interview.

items.title: Some Australians fear lockdowns. Economists say stagflation is the bigger risk

children.content: As fuel costs surge and supply fears grow, businesses and households say the economic shock from the war in the Middle East is already hitting home.

image.altText: A price board displaying petrol for more than $2.47 per litre.

items.title: Refinery fire won't bump Australia to stage three of fuel plan: PM

children.content: Anthony Albanese says the fire at the Geelong refinery won't lead to a change in Australia's fuel security plan. Follow live.

image.altText: a phone photo of smoke lit up on the skyline behind a Viva Energy sign

items.title: Australia’s fuel supply: Where things stand and what we’re watching

children.content: We know you have to get where you're going this weekend. ABC News is here to help with a round-up of all the key news on fuel price and supply in Australia.

image.altText: Fuel nozzle with dollar sign

items.title: What we know about the 'small fire' that became an 'unprecedented' Geelong refinery blaze

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

articles.title: Poor communication, no updates during oil refinery fire, residents claim

children.content: Geelong residents near an oil refinery fire have told a community meeting that initial information from emergency services was misleading and there was a lack of updates.

image.altText: Smoke near a refinery.

items.title: In charts: A look at Australia's record spending on defence

children.content: The Defence Department reveals where the money will be going as Australia plans to spend a record $425 billion over the next decade.

image.altText: Minister for Defence Industry Pat Conroy and Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles in front of a submarine.

items.title: Australia is on the verge of aged care AI boom

children.content: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

image.altText: Andromeda's Abi robot

items.title: Migration debate deserves less politicking from the Liberals

children.content: The Liberals need to resolve the internal ambiguities they have on big policy questions when it comes to migration.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands behind Jonno Duniam.

items.title: Coca-Cola flags it could pull out of Qld recycling scheme

children.content: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

image.altText: Coke cans at a WA containers for change depot

items.title: Mother of 13 who 'never thought we’d be homeless' pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

items.title: Australian billionaire Andrew Forrest battles Meta over fake ads

children.content: Australian billionaire mining magnate Andrew Forrest is asking a US federal court in Silicon Valley to hold Meta accountable for scam ads using his likeness without permission.

image.altText: A side-on photo of a man wearing a blue suit, white shirt and yellow tie gesturing with his right hand while speaking on stage.

items.title: AFL-backed helmet company pulls concussion claims after ABC report

children.content: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

image.altText: A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: Are you growing Australia’s next invasive weeds in your garden?

children.content: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown

items.title: How mobile phone cameras have helped unearth a mysterious kingdom

children.content: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

image.altText: A cluster of cup-shaped mushrooms cling to a damp fallen branch amonst feathery ferns.

items.title: Elderly couple beg council to let them stay in Gold Coast caravan park

children.content: Time is running out for residents facing eviction from Kirra Beach Tourist Park. In 2018, the council told them they needed to move out by 2028 to make way for tourists, but with rising rent and property prices, they say they cannot afford to live anywhere else.

gallery.altText: man and woman with dog sitting on lap

thumbnail.altText: man and woman with dog sitting on lap

items.title: Wall Street pushes record highs

children.content: Wall Street markets post record highs despite the uncertainty of the Middle East conflict. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Pope blasts 'tyrants' spending big on war following public spat with Trump

children.content: The US-born pope has praised peace movements and peace makers during his visit to the African nation of Cameroon as the rift with US President Donald Trump intensifies.

image.altText: A man in white robes is surrounded by people and cameras as he waves

items.title: Decision looms as Ben Roberts-Smith expected to apply for bail

children.content: Australia's most decorated living soldier has spent more than a week in custody. Today in court, he is expected to make a bid to be released on bail after being charged with war crimes.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: 'Same general, new clothes': Civilian facade as military hides wealth in Thailand

children.content: As the Myanmar military carried out air strikes during a brutal civil war, the junta chief's family quietly purchased luxury real estate in Thailand.

image.altText: man in yellow silk cap.

items.title: 'Plate to paddock': How a festival's waste became a farmer's treasure

children.content: Tonnes of organic waste from the National Folk Festival are being turned into fertiliser on a farm outside Canberra in a circular economy project delivering more benefits than just waste reduction.

items.title: 'Shattered' Blue feels he let Carlton down in narrow loss to Collingwood

children.content: Carlton coach Michael Voss says the club will support Elijah Hollands after his performance against Collingwood last night.

image.altText: Michael Voss and Elijah Hollands after Carlton's defeat against Collingwood, 2026

items.title: Trbojevic injures hamstring as Manly thrash Cowboys

children.content: The Sea Eagles have smashed North Queensland but the win has come at a heavy cost following a hamstring injury to star fullback Tom Trbojevic.

image.altText: Tom Trbojevic looks despondent after suffering an injury

items.title: 'Upset and angry': WA community shocked by rapist's bail status

children.content: Wayne Jeffrey Doherty remained in the Broome community for five months after pleading guilty to rape.

image.altText: A man with a cowboy hat, sunglasses and beard

items.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism

children.content: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars earlier in the year.

image.altText: Coco Gauff smashes her racquet in CCTV video.

items.title: Alpine ash endangered listing 'will do nothing to protect them'

children.content: Scientists hope an endangered listing may help preserve alpine ash forests, but the forestry industry is raising concerns that it could result in further decline.

gallery.altText: Aerial view of burnt pine forests.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of burnt pine forests.

items.title: Mandelson failed vetting but was given UK ambassador to US job anyway

children.content: Peter Mandelson was sacked from the role after revelations about his connections with the late sex offender became public.

image.altText: Peter Mandelson, wearing a blue suit, speaks in front of the UK and US flags.

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

image.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: Australian bush tea hits shelves in Asia

children.content: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

image.altText: A close up image of tea leaves in a pot.

items.title: Airport workers angered by surprise Qantas route cuts

children.content: Qantas's decision to cut one regional South Australian route and suspend four others came as a shock to workers at regional airports.

image.altText: A QantasLink plane on the tarmac

items.title: How Aled Jones recorded Walking in the Air almost by pure accident in 1985

children.content: From learning Walking in the Air on the fly to working with Leonard Bernstein, Aled Jones is currently touring Australia and shares some of his most colourful stories.
